channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Hantavirus countdown: U.S. cruise passengers settle in for 42 days of waiting
item.description: The hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship has potentially exposed passengers to a deadly disease. Most returning Americans are now housed in Nebraska. Some may be cleared to quarantine at home.
item.content:encoded:
The hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship has potentially exposed passengers to a deadly disease. Most returning Americans are now housed in Nebraska. Some may be cleared to quarantine at home. 

(Image credit: Dylan Widger)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Son reflects with his mother about growing up with autism
item.description: Jhovana Figueroa was diagnosed with autism when he was a toddler. For StoryCorps, Figueroa and his mom talk about his childhood and their hopes for the future.
item.content:encoded: Jhovana Figueroa was diagnosed with autism when he was a toddler. For StoryCorps, Figueroa and his mom talk about his childhood and their hopes for the future.
item.dc:creator: Esther Honig

item.title: The Supreme Court keeps abortion pill mifepristone available by telehealth
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.

(Image credit: Jose Luis Magana)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Linda McMahon defends dismantling the Education Department, shifting its work
item.description: The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.
item.content:encoded:
The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.

(Image credit: Eric Lee)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: A brain-controlled system may help listeners with hearing loss cut through the noise
item.description: A hearing system that monitors brain waves could help people with hearing loss communicate in noisy environments.
item.content:encoded:
A hearing system that monitors brain waves could help people with hearing loss communicate in noisy environments.

(Image credit: Matteo Farinella)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: The MAHA movement is coming to school cafeterias. Here's what that means for kids
item.description: U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: French hantavirus patient is critically ill as outbreak grows to 11
item.description: A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.
item.content:encoded:
A French woman infected in the deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is critically ill and being treated with an artificial lung. The outbreak has now reached 11 total reported cases, 9 of which have been confirmed.

(Image credit: Michel Euler)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: About 7 million kids live in a home with a loaded and unlocked gun, a study finds
item.description: The new study in JAMA Network Open also finds that more parents leave their guns loaded and unlocked when they have teenagers, despite the fact that suicide risk goes up for this age group.
item.content:encoded:
The new study in JAMA Network Open also finds that more parents leave their guns loaded and unlocked when they have teenagers, despite the fact that suicide risk goes up for this age group.

(Image credit: Charles Krupa)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: FDA commissioner resigns after tumultuous tenure
item.description: The resignation ends Dr. Marty Makary's management of the Food and Drug Administration, which was marked by turmoil and controversy.
item.content:encoded:
The resignation ends Dr. Marty Makary's management of the Food and Drug Administration, which was marked by turmoil and controversy.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: She's trying to outrun pancreatic cancer. Breakthrough treatments give her hope
item.description: Pancreatic cancer is notoriously lethal. But new treatments mean that may be changing, and people with the disease now have more reason to hope than ever before.
item.content:encoded:
Pancreatic cancer is notoriously lethal. But new treatments mean that may be changing, and people with the disease now have more reason to hope than ever before.

(Image credit: Gavin McIntyre for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi
